name: Aged mice showed delayed resolution rescued by nano-proresolving medicines
summary: Aged mice had delayed acute-inflammation resolution and reduced SPMs; DHA and aspirin-triggered resolvin nano-proresolving medicines activated resolution pathways.
statement: In aged mice, acute inflammation resolution was delayed and SPMs were reduced; DHA accelerated resolution through increased SPMs, while nano-proresolving medicines carrying aspirin-triggered resolvins reduced inflammation by promoting efferocytosis.
population_or_model: Aged mice in self-resolving zymosan peritonitis, with human monocyte mechanistic assays
quantitative_note: The finding supports an aging-linked resolution deficit and pharmacologic resolution activation, not human tissue-repair efficacy.
caveats: The in vivo efficacy evidence is animal-level.
Peritonitis resolution is not the same as durable tissue repair or regeneration.
The nano-proresolving medicine delivery approach is experimental.
